nameMeanings.name: Wolferton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Wolferton in Norfolk. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfhere + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Reaney has suggested that the surname could also derive from any of the places called Wollerton, Wolverton, and Woolverton (see 2 below), yet there is very little evidence of this surname outside of East Anglia from the 15th century onward. It therefore appears that the Norfolk place is the most likely origin of present-day surname.

perhaps sometimes a habitational name from Wollerton (Shropshire), Wolverton (Buckinghamshire, Hampshire, Warwickshire, Worcestershire), and Woolverton (Somerset). The Shropshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfrūn + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Buckinghamshire, Hampshire, Worcestershire, and Somerset placenames derive from the Old English personal name Wulfhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Warwickshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wulfweard + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton was born in 1350, in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. She married Roger de Wolverstone in 1370, in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton est née en 1350, à Woolverstone, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Roger de Wolverstone en 1370, à Woolverstone, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton wurde 1350 in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat 1370 in Woolverstone, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Roger de Wolverstone geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton è nata il nel 1350 nel Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Roger de Wolverstone nel 1370, nel Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolfertonは1350年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウルバーストーンで生まれた。1370年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウルバーストーンでRoger de Wolverstoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton(은)는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 보버스톤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1370년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 보버스톤에서 Roger de Wolverstone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton nasceu em 1350, em Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Roger de Wolverstone em 1370, em Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton родилась в 1350 г. в местности вулвирстоун, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Wolverstone в 1370 г. в местности вулвирстоун, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Beatrice de Wolferton nació en 1350, en Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Roger de Wolverstone en 1370, en Woolverstone, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo.
